items.title: Confirm 72-hour drinking water is set aside
items.detail: Count sealed bottles and containers so each person has at least three litres per day.

items.title: Pack no-cook or quick-cook meals
items.detail: Favour food that can be eaten cold if power or safe cooking space is lost.

items.title: Pack pet food and pet water
items.detail: Do not assume pet needs can be covered from household drinking reserves.

items.title: Place food tools with the pantry
items.detail: Keep the manual can opener, utensils, and paper towels with stored food.

items.title: Review the fridge and freezer use plan
items.detail: Know what to use first and when chilled food should be discarded.

items.title: Charge phones, radios, and power banks
items.detail: Start any forecast event with the highest possible battery reserve.

items.title: Keep the radio station list with the radio
items.detail: Do not rely on memory when stress and outages make simple tasks harder.

items.title: Place vehicle chargers in the car
items.detail: Keep backup charging available if the car becomes the main power source.

items.title: Print or laminate the contact sheet
items.detail: Store one copy in a go-bag and one copy at home.

items.title: Download or pack offline maps
items.detail: Make sure at least one route reference works without mobile coverage.

items.title: Check medicine buffer and repeats
items.detail: Refill essential prescriptions before high-risk periods if possible.

items.title: Restock the first aid kit
items.detail: Replace used dressings, expired medicines, and flat torch batteries.

items.title: Update the medical summary
items.detail: List conditions, allergies, medications, and emergency contacts on paper.

items.title: Place IDs and policies in the document pouch
items.detail: Include copies of licences, insurance, and important account details.

items.title: Pack spare glasses and essential aids
items.detail: Store backup glasses, chargers, hearing aid batteries, or mobility items together.

items.title: Stage go-bags by the exit
items.detail: Put shoes, clothing, torches, water, and medications where they are easy to grab.

items.title: Check fuel and tyre condition
items.detail: A safe vehicle is part of the plan even if you intend to shelter first.

items.title: Set out pet carriers and leads
items.detail: Do not wait until the last minute to find restraint gear.

items.title: Confirm any neighbour check-in plan
items.detail: Know who may need assistance and how you will check on them safely.

items.title: Review meeting points and departure triggers
items.detail: Make sure everyone knows where to go and when the plan changes from shelter to move.
